title: Production main() HTTP handlers are untested — test suite reimplements the routes
evidence: app_test.go:60 newTestRouter() re-declares all six route closures instead of exercising main()'s router (app.go:213-396); go test coverage = 26.3% (scores.json test_coverage=0.263). A routing/binding/status-code bug in main() would not be caught.
suggestion: Extract handler construction into a shared func(db *Database) *gin.Engine used by both main() and the tests, so tests cover the real router.

title: ~180 lines of handler logic duplicated between app.go and app_test.go
evidence: app.go:213-396 and app_test.go:60-233 are near-identical route registrations; idiomatic score 0.42 (scores.json). Divergence risk: e.g. app_test.go:100 returns a different error body than app.go:261.
suggestion: Register routes once in a factory function shared by production and tests.

title: PUT uses zero-value sentinels, so a client cannot set year=0 or clear a text field
evidence: app.go:341-356 treats empty title/author/isbn and year==0 as 'keep existing', so PUT behaves as a partial merge and cannot reset a field to its zero value; PUT also skips the title/author required-validation that POST enforces.
suggestion: Use pointer fields (*string/*int) in UpdateBookRequest to distinguish 'omitted' from 'set to zero', and revalidate required fields.

title: Build artifact and test DB left in workspace
evidence: book-api (15.9MB compiled binary) plus test_books.db/-shm/-wal are present in run_dir alongside source.
suggestion: Add a .gitignore for the binary and *.db* files; not a code defect.
